MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEFFREY WINDSOR, TEL. NO. 1-613-632-4500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. There are triable issues whether CCSF breached its duty of care and whether that breach was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff Luz Godizano's claimed injuries. CCSF is a common carrier and required to use the utmost care and diligence for the safe carriage of a person. (Civil Code 2100; Lopez v. Southern Cal. Rapid Transit Dist. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 785.) "Such carriers are responsible for any, even the slightest, negligence and are required to do all that human care, vigilance, and foresight reasonably can do under the circumstances to avoid harm to passengers." (Acosta v. Southern Cal. Rapid Transit Dist. (1970) 2 Cal.3d 19, 27.) Whether Mr. Zamudio acted in a reasonably prudent manner under the circumstances is a factual issue. (Aboudara v. San Francisco (1952) 114 Cal.App.2d 630, 632.) Mr. Zamudio admits that when he approached the intersection, he did not see whether cars were waiting to turn left. (Feeney Dec., Ex. A (Zamudio Depo., 77:12-18); Windsor Dec., Ex. B (Zamudio Depo., 82:23-83:20.) A bus operator is taught to slow down and cover his or her brakes before entering an intersection. (Zamudio Depo., 110:4-7; Feeney Dec., Ex. B (Wilson Depo., 52:3-10; 70:9-12; 79:10-80:10).) There is a reasonable inference that Mr. Zamudio did not comply with this directive as he proceeded downhill towards the intersection. (Wilson Depo., 139:20-141:10.) CCSF's objections to Mr. Wilson's testimony are overruled. As a training supervisor, he can testify regarding what CCSF teaches bus operators and it is not improper lay opinion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk
